feed.title: SAP Community - Careers
feed.subtitle: Careers blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Elevate Your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Architect for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Please post your quesiton in our SAP Learning Q&A  forum.

In the rapidly evolving technology landscape, businesses are constantly searching for ways to stay ahead of the competition and drive innovation. For IT professionals, this shift presents a golden opportunity: becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect. By mastering SAP Business Technology Platform, a unified, integrated offering that transforms the way organizations approach digital transformation, you position yourself as a pivotal force in helping organizations unlock new efficiencies, innovate faster, and achieve their business goals. 

Your pathway to success 

As companies rapidly shift to cloud-based solutions, there is an increasing demand for skilled professionals who can bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. As SAP BTP Solution Architect, you play a crucial role in this transformation journey, guiding businesses through integration, extension, analytics, and data management. And with a brand-new role-based certification, we are helping you to achieve this goal.

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Professional - Solution Architect - SAP BTP. This certification validates that you possess the ability to perform an SAP Solution Architect role with SAP Business Technology Platform. By getting certified, you demonstrate that you can develop and implement solutions that meet business requirements and translate requirements of an organization into technical solution designs.

Start with this free learning journey 

Getting started is easy. Just look at the new free, self-paced learning journey "Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect" on the SAP Learning site. After completing this learning journey, you will have a comprehensive understanding of the tools and methods required to effectively use SAP BTP to envision and design an enterprise architecture. You will be equipped with the skills to align IT strategies with business objectives using SAP BTP tools and technologies.  

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and maximize your learning experience by joining the SAP Learning Groups, to ask questions from SAP experts and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: Build the Skills to Bring out Your Best: SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning Zone Is Now Live!
entry.content:
Calling all tech enthusiasts! With SAP TechEd Virtual being just around the corner, it’s time to get ahead of the game and build the skills to bring out your best. Join a community of experts, acquire in-demand skills, and prepare for a globally recognized certification with self-paced learning in the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone – even before the main event starts on October 8!

Learning tools and opportunities for you:

  • Get started with 40 interactive value journeys and showcases to discover the value of SAP solutions.
  • Access 45 free, self-paced learning journeys curated to each of the content tracks of SAP TechEd Virtual to upskill in the latest SAP solutions. 
  • Subscribe to premium, guided learning resources with SAP Learning Hub to maximize your skills and earn a globally recognized certification, with an exclusive 25% discount.
  • Access demos and 25 basic trials to explore the power of SAP solutions and 50 use cases to get hands-on experience.
  • Connect with experts, expand your network, and collaborate with peers in dedicated learning groups and bridge the time until October 8 with SAP Community events.

Explore learning offerings for each SAP TechEd track:

  • Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Application Development and Automation
  • Data and Analytics
  • Integration

Register for SAP TechEd Virtual to benefit from a 25% discount on SAP Learning Hub:

 As a registered SAP TechEd Virtual attendee, you can benefit from an exclusive 25% discount on your SAP Learning Hub subscription! Once you have subscribed to our guided premium learning offering, you can get access to learning content and certification exam attempts, expert guidance in digital live sessions and on-demand hands-on practice systems too.

We know that learning is vital – now more than ever. Jump to the SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone today to get a head start into SAP TechEd in 2024.

See you all at SAP TechEd Virtual – Learning zone, and beyond!

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Amin Omidy How Lifelong Learning Can Unlock Your Career Potential
entry.content:
@Amin_Omidyhas been reading SAP white papers and SAP Community posts for as long as he can remember, covering various areas of interest for the Solution and Enterprise Architect who works as a consultant for clients. “I am always fascinated by the rich information and great answers to different questions related to SAP implementation”, he explains. “I subscribed to different topics to receive notifications via email and actively search for mature SAP answers or related SAP notes to help me with my issues.” 

With more than ten years of experience in SAP solutions for both private and public sectors, Amin certainly knows the pain points of his customers – and how to solve them. “My expertise lies in helping businesses transition to the cloud and establishing modern ERP implementations. This can involve starting an ERP project from scratch or enhancing existing customer solutions, such as migrating on-premise solutions to the cloud or integrating new Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) applications into their IT landscape”, he says. Where else but in the SAP Community could he find answers to such complex issues?  

SAP Learning site helps solve complex problems

Amin: "I am always fascinated by the rich information and great answers the SAP Community provides related to SAP implementation."Well, there’s also the SAP Learning site, Amin admits. “Your website offers a wealth of up-to-date and practical insights, making it an invaluable resource for staying current with the latest SAP developments and best practices.” What he values the most, according to the Canadian resident, is the structured and detailed content helping him solve complex problems and enhancing his understanding of SAP solutions. “My passion to enable businesses to streamline their operations drives me to stay updated with the latest technological advancements”, he concludes.  

However, it’s not only his passion towards his customers that drives Amin to stay at the forefront of innovation. He is convinced: “Living in an era dominated by IT technologies like VMs, microservices, cloud computing, and AI, continuous learning has become essential.” According to Amin, enablement and engagement provided from SAP has significantly enhanced his ability to serve customers better. “Each new piece of knowledge or technique I acquire not only boosts my professional skills but also brings a sense of accomplishment when applied in real-world scenarios.” Watching the positive results and customer satisfaction would confirm the value of lifelong learning and its impact on both his career and personal growth.  

Making impact as SAP Champion

Maybe that’s one of the reasons why Amin also enjoys mentoring young professionals and participating in webinars to share knowledge and learn from others, even outside of work. And why he became an SAP Champion. He simply loves engaging with others, be it his customers or peers in the SAP Community. “Whether through writing blogs, addressing customer issues and potential solutions or serving as a voice for customers within the SAP ecosystem, it allows me to actively contribute and make a meaningful impact”, he recently stated in an interview.  

In return, Amin gets a lot back: “Learning something new through the SAP Community has been incredibly rewarding. The reliability and depth of the information provided are unmatched, ensuring that I can trust the knowledge I gain and apply it directly to my daily work. The opportunity to interact with bloggers or writers to verify any part of the answer or get more related details is particularly valuable. This engagement helps me stay updated with the latest best practices and solutions, enhancing my skills and efficiency in handling SAP-related tasks. The experience is both exciting and encouraging, continually expanding my understanding and capabilities within the SAP ecosystem.” 

Start your own success story today

If you want to stay informed, connected, and up to date with the latest innovations, explore the SAP Community and head over to SAP Learning site to quickly learn a new skill, upskill, and get certified. For access to our guided, premium learning resources, consider a subscription to SAP Learning Hub.

entry.title: New Study Quantifies the Benefits of Continuous Learning with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
For businesses, ensuring business success in times of rapid digital transformation isn’t easy. But if you work in the technology sector, staying up to date with the latest innovations is equally important for you and your career growth. While there are many ways to upskill or reskill, many professionals turn to SAP learning solutions. And that’s great – here’s why: 

A recent study now* shows that upskilling with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now helps customers gain the right skills critical to drive digital transformation – while also ensuring project success and maximizing the return on investment your organizations made with SAP solutions. Keep reading to learn more about why future-focused SAP learning solutions could be the right path for you and your employer and how SAP customers have benefited from their investment. 

Who conducted the study and how was it done? 

The study on the power of continuous upskilling with SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now was conducted by Forrester Consulting, a leading global market research company, on behalf of SAP in 2024. As organizations undergo digital transformation, we sought to understand how our offerings are helping customers and partners to ensure business success and examine the potential return on their investment (ROI) by using SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now.  

For their research, Forrester used their Total Economic Impact (TEI) methodology, a trusted industry standard that models all aspects of a technology or solution and the associated impacts on the business. Focusing on quantifying benefits, Forrester analyzed costs, flexibility and more. Through interviews with 12 experienced SAP customers, they provided a comprehensive study backed by solid data on the value of SAP’s offerings. 

What problems did learners and organizations have? 

Interviewees said that prior to using SAP Enable Now or adopting SAP Learning Hub, their organizations relied on legacy approaches to provide training and enablement content for their employees. Advanced SAP users such as project leads, and IT team members even lacked quality SAP-specific content and internal learning tools. In-person, instructor-led classes and onsite consultants were valuable but not realistic for all employees given the time and cost commitments. These limitations led to knowledge gaps and inefficiencies for typical end users and advanced users alike. 

What changed for learners after SAP Learning Hub or SAP Enable Now were implemented? 

As one training lead from the transportation sector put it: “SAP Learning Hub has all the resources that you need in one system. Our employees can upskill and also get the promotion they want.” In solid data points, Forrester presented the following benefits of using SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now to upskill: 

  • Improved upskilling due to greater learning access, resulting in productivity gains of up to 25%: As a source of extensive, high-quality, and digitally accessible guided SAP learning resources for those in more expert roles, SAP Learning Hub equipped users with the knowledge they need to stay up to speed and learn new skills. 
  • Faster time to competence by 50% for end users: SAP Enable Now provided end users at the composite organization representative of interviewed customers with updated, easy-to-follow content and assistance directly in the flow of work.  

Interviewees reported that after the investment in SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now, their workforce became more knowledgeable, confident, and productive. Key results from the investment included improved upskilling for advanced SAP users, cost savings, faster time to competence for end users, more efficient content creation, and greater project success. 

How can you benefit from SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable Now? 

That’s easy! If you’re looking for future-focused, high-quality, and digitally accessible upskilling, you can rest assured that SAP Learning Hub is the right thing to turn to. With a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub, you can get access to all resources you need to prepare for certification, take the exams with four included attempts and stay certified – at better value than ever before.  

Where can I learn more about getting and staying certified with SAP Learning Hub? 

To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ. Take a deep dive into the Forrester TEI study, click here. To learn more about SAP Learning Hub, visit this page. 

*July 2024 Forrester Consulting case study commissioned by SAP titled: The Total Economic Impact ™ of SAP Learning Hub and SAP Enable now. Read the full study. Results are for a composite organization.

entry.title: Hear from SAP Champion Kunal Bansal 🏅: How SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 Can Help Boost Your Career
entry.content:
When @KunalBansal booked his hotel in Bangalore near the 2023 SAP TechEd venue, he knew he wouldn’t be spending much time there. Instead, his real focus was on the learning, networking, and upskilling at SAP’s premier technology event - TechEd. “Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says, reflecting on his experience. Attending SAP TechEd in-person for the 2nd time, he discovered just how powerful in-person networking and knowledge-sharing could be. “It’s the best place to connect, ask questions, and exchange ideas,” Kunal emphasizes. 

His enthusiasm shines through as he recalls the inspiring keynote delivered by SAP Board Members, which focused on the transformative potential of GenAI. He also cherishes the time spent at the SAP Community booth, engaging with fellow SAP Mentors and Champions. “I’m definitely attending again this year,” he says with a smile. 

With SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 on October 8-9 just weeks away, Kunal is busy curating his agenda. He’s carefully selecting sessions that will provide the most value, both professionally and personally. 

Kunal's Top Picks for SAP TechEd Virtual in 2024:

  • XP101 | SAP BTP strategy and highlights  
    Get firsthand insights into SAP’s BTP strategy and gain a deeper understanding of how SAP BTP unifies development, automation, data, and AI into one powerful platform, driving digital transformation. 
  • IN100 | Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite 
    Discover how to accelerate innovation by integrating business systems using SAP Integration Suite, enabling real-time, scalable enterprise-wide integration.  
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite 
    Learn how to transition smoothly from legacy systems to the SAP cloud, leveraging SAP Integration Suite’s robust tools, best practices and services for a seamless migration. 

Make sure you also check out Kunal's entire expert session playlist for SAP TechEd Virtual!

SAP Learning site is his go-to resource

“Learn, share, grow - that’s the motto I believe in,” Kunal says.During the week, Kunal is fully immersed in his role as an Advisory Architect at IBM, leveraging over 13 years of experience in SAP CX Suites. His deep expertise in SAP Intelligent Enterprise and SAP Solutions underscores his commitment to staying current in the field.

“I work closely with stakeholders to deliver scalable, compliant, and high-performing solutions using SAP technologies,” Kunal explains. His role demands a unique blend of technical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong communication skills to drive successful project outcomes. “And to stay current, I dedicate at least an hour daily to learning.” 

Kunal often turns to the SAP Learning site to stay updated with the rapidly evolving technologies. “The free SAP Learning portal has been my go-to resource for learning journeys, leading to SAP Certification, while Learning Hub and Partner Edge portals have deepened my concepts to deliver greater business value for clients". 

Strengthened credibility, solidified reputation

While dedicating an hour a day to learning can seem daunting at first, Kunal says it’s worth it, as it has become a crucial part of his journey, pushing him to achieve greater heights and inspiring others. “Earning SAP Certifications through continuous learning has been a game-changer for my career growth, unlocking opportunities for more complex, high-impact projects”. On a more personal level, Kunal notes, “SAP Certifications have strengthened my credibility and solidified my reputation as a thought leader in the SAP Community.” 

As Kunal finalizes his SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 agenda, he’s also exploring the recently launched SAP TechEd Virtual Learning Zone to discover further learning tools and opportunities mapped to the six SAP TechEd Virtual content tracks. “I’m excited to dive into Developing with SAP Integration Suite to keep myself engaged until SAP TechEd Virtual kicks off,” Kunal says, eagerly anticipating the innovations in SAP Business AI and SAP BTP. 

Now it's your time to shine!

What sessions are you most excited about, and what’s your favorite resource from the SAP TechEd Virtual - Learning Zone? Share your thoughts in the comments and make sure to visit the SAP TechEd group on SAP Community to connect with peers!

entry.title: Heatwave of Innovation: A Glimpse Into the 2024 Premium Hub CoE STEM High School Summer Orientation
entry.content:
This summer, a group of bright high school students embarked on an exciting journey at SAP's week-long STEM orientation program. This program focused on harnessing the power of cloud-based AI apps using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), providing students with practical, hands-on learning experiences using low-code/no-code tools.

 

The week kicked off with students registering for their SAP accounts and setting up trial accounts on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). A key component of the learning experience was SAP Build Apps, a powerful tool within SAP BTP that enabled students to design and develop applications using a low-code/no-code approach. This tool simplified the app creation process, allowing students to focus on functionality and creativity rather than complex coding.

The primary task was to create a barcode scanner application using SAP Build Apps. This app scanned barcodes and retrieved detailed information about food items from the Open Food Facts API, a free and open database of food product information. This integration taught students how to connect their applications to external data sources, demonstrating the potential of combining SAP BTP with other digital resources.

 

 

The real magic happened when students were introduced to Google Gemini, allowing them to enhance their applications with Generative AI capabilities. By integrating AI into their apps, students were able to analyze the health aspects of food ingredients, transforming simple barcode scanners into intelligent, interactive applications. This exercise encouraged students to think critically about how AI can enhance user experiences and provide valuable insights.

As the week progressed, students eagerly worked on their projects, each bringing their own flair to the task. They showcased the culmination of their hard work in a demo session held on Friday, presenting their enhanced applications. It was inspiring to see how each participant leveraged the tools and guidance provided to create something truly remarkable.

The orientation week was not just about building apps; it was about fostering a spirit of innovation and collaboration. The students left with more than just technical skills; they gained confidence and a deeper understanding of how technology can be used to solve real-world problems.

The feedback from participants has been overwhelmingly positive, with many expressing how the program has sparked a new interest in technology and AI. Here's what one of our students had to say about their unforgettable experience:

Participating in this program was an invaluable experience. I had the chance to meet many new people and delve deeply into SAP, low-coding program development, and generative AI. This program pushed me out of my comfort zone and provided a valuable glimpse into the future of IT. I appreciated the opportunity to grow, learn, and envision a career in this dynamic field. - Riddhi Bakde

This orientation was a testament to the power of education and technology in shaping the minds of tomorrow's innovators. As we continue to provide these opportunities, we are excited to see how these young minds will contribute to the ever-evolving tech landscape.

 

Thank you to Balaji Rao Gaddam for sponsoring and hosting this year’s event.  

Thank you to the SAP colleagues who coordinated this year’s Summer Orientation and made it a success!

Ashok Kumar M & Megan Rech (Leads) Jared Durkin (Project Co-Coordinator), Luke Korkuis (HR) and volunteers and speakers. A huge thank you to our onsite team coordinators Oswayne Smith, Nkem Ohanenye, Cecily Devaney, Nivi Rao and Jacy Yu.

And a special thanks to the SAP speakers and colleagues who shared their expertise!
John Astill (D-Shop Demo), Evan Taylor (STAR Internship Program), Justin Lynch (SAP Overview & Career Development), George Harwood (NSQ Campus Tour), Safwanur Rahman & Vivek RR (Artificial Intelligence Experts), Rakesh Gandhi and Matt Ruff (Industry 4.0 Center Tour), Manuel Gruber (MCC Presentation)

 

If you have any questions or feedback about the Summer Orientation, please contact Megan Rech.

entry.title: New SAP SuccessFactors Certifications for Certified Individuals in SAP HCM (C_THR12) Available
entry.content:
The shift to cloud computing has undoubtedly changed the way organizations carry out their HR operations. For SAP users, the cloud-based future of Human Capital Management lies in the SAP SuccessFactors solution.

That is also why the maintenance period for SAP Business Suite 7 (ECC) and the previous SAP ERP HCM system as an on-premise solution ends on December 31, 2027. If you are already operating the SAP HCM solution in the Compatibility Pack (CP) with SAP S/4HANA, the usage rights will end on December 31, 2025. As a result, the SAP Certification SAP Certified Associate - SAP HCM for S/4HANA (C_THR12) will be discontinued on March 31, 2025. You can find the planned retirement date on the certification website. Certified users will stay certified for 12 months after the certification retirement. The final due date will be visible on My Learning Certifications (sap.com). Please also note that the academies THR10 and THR12 a– the trainings to prepare for the certification, will be discontinued by the end of Q1, 2025. 

But don’t worry about the retirement! To ensure you stay on top of your HR game, SAP stepped up and continues to launch future-focused earning and enablement resources for you, making sure you excel in a cloud-first HR world. On the SAP Learning site, you can find tailored, bite-sized learning and certification opportunities for the SAP SuccessFactors solution, including topics like recruiting, workforce analytics and planning, talent management, payroll, and more. A few highlights include: 

Learning journeys for beginners: 

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core 
  • Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors Platform 

 SAP Certifications:

  • SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Compensation 

To learn more about certification retirements, check out this FAQ. To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ. Browse all available SAP Certifications here.

entry.title: Accelerating Digital Transformation: An Insider's Journey to SAP Cloud ALM Mastery
entry.content:
“It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, Martina Ohlerth says. The IT engineer and product owner works at REWE digital in Germany and oversees the further development of the cloud-based tool for application lifecycle management (ALM), which is available as part of SAP support offerings, covering all stages of the application lifecycle, from discovery of cloud solution capabilities to implementation, operations, and continuous innovation as well as business value realization and enhancement.

“We wanted to preserve our investment with SAP Cloud ALM” 

 With more than 18 years of experience in the field, the 42-year-old has experienced digital transformation first-hand. “To date, we have already achieved a lot with SAP Solution Manager to be more transparent in application lifecycle management. We want to preserve this investment with SAP Cloud ALM.”, Martina explains. “This is the main reason why we are intensively investigating SAP's new tool." 

The mother of three and her small team of four colleagues that is sometimes supported by external consultants embarked on the SAP Cloud ALM journey one and a half years ago. To learn more about the strategy behind the solution, and to find out if it was the right fit for the company, Martina turned to the German SAP-user group DSAG and one of their conferences. “This helped us a lot to find out where we could be going with this”, she explains. 

First learning journey developed with SAP partner  

Liking what they saw, the team began with training for the standard processes. “We turned to live sessions at SAP, looked at the SAP Community and more to get the latest information”, Martina says. When she heard about the beginner-level learning journey “Implementing with SAP Cloud ALM”, available on the SAP Learning site, she immediately saw the potential for everyone on her team to benefit from the free learning resource.  

Created together with SAP partner Vostura, the free, self-paced course is the first that has been developed in collaboration, allowing learners to gain all the necessary knowledge to use SAP Cloud ALM in agile implementation projects – in just under 10 hours. The activities were the starting point for the team's participation in the classroom training “SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation“ to gain direct experience through extensive system exercises with SAP Cloud ALM. 

“In my line of business, it is vital to stay up-to-date with the latest innovations”, Martina highlights, recommending continuous learning for everyone, not just her team. “It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, she concludes.   

What’s in it for you? 

Are you looking to boost your career and kick-start your own success story? Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Celebrating Milestones with SAP Learning Hub: Your One-Stop-Shop for Upskilling and Career Growth
entry.content:
It’s only been a few months since SAP officially launched the enhanced SAP Learning Hub – but the success of the new premium, guided learning offering already shows that it’s exactly what future-oriented learners are looking for. So, let’s celebrate a couple of huge milestones with us today and learn more about why SAP Learning Hub is your one-stop-shop to learn a new skill, get certified, and boost your career in times of rapid technological innovations!

One Subscription is all You Need

In case you didn’t know it yet: The launch of the enhanced SAP Learning Hub marked a major step in the evolution of all things SAP solution learning. Knowing that tech changes faster than ever before – let’s just think about AI for a second! –, SAP professionals and those aspiring to become one need to quickly gain new skills, adapt to changing environments, and get certified faster to prove their expertise to (future) employers. That’s what the enhanced SAP Learning Hub is here for.

Providing access to great content, expert guidance in digital live sessions, on-demand hands-on practice systems, advanced analytics and learning management functionalities, plus four included SAP Certification exam attempts per learner per year, a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub ensures you stay relevant and ahead of the game. And apparently, it pays off.

Favorite Live Session Topic Comes as No Surprise

With more than 150,000 registrants in live sessions in 2024 so far, the popularity of this offer is increasing rapidly and continuously. And the year isn’t over yet! But with hundreds of sessions to choose from each month, opportunities to engage with live experts and participants from all over the world, we couldn’t be happier about the results!

And if you ever wondered about the most popular live session topics, take a look at the current Top 5 – which one is your favorite?

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
  • GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, ABAP Environment

New Type of Practice System and More Enhancements

When you’re diving into a new SAP solution, it’s important to get a good first look at the software and how it’s running, right? That’s where another amazing feature of the enhanced SAP Learning Hub comes in: Practice systems.  There are more than 340 systems available to you – plus two sandbox systems – to explore, test, experiment and build use cases with your SAP solution.

And as we know you like to take your time exploring, we extended the system availability to 21 days for a couple of longer courses. Cool, huh? (If you want to learn more about practice systems, I highly recommend checking out this podcast).

And if you are interested in the most popular topics in SAP Learning Hub – let us unveil the secret: It’s of course SAP S/4HANA Cloud, both Private and Public Edition, and SAP’s future-ready enterprise resource planning (ERP) system. But SAP SuccessFactors is also very popular among users.

Still unsure about the benefits of SAP Learning Hub?

Then look no further! A recent study from Forrester now shows that upskilling with SAP Learning Hub helps customers gain the right skills critical to drive digital transformation – while also ensuring project success and maximizing the return on investment your organizations made with SAP solutions.

Forrester presented the following benefits of using SAP Learning Hub to upskill:

  • Improved upskilling due to greater learning access, resulting in productivity gains of up to 25%: As a source of extensive, high-quality, and digitally accessible guided SAP learning resources for those in more expert roles, SAP Learning Hub equipped users with the knowledge they need to stay up to speed and learn new skills.
  • Cost savings of 50% with digital delivery. Organizations find SAP Learning Hub to be a cost-effective solution that users can access anytime for self-paced training. It complements the organization’s existing training methods, which ultimately results in cost savings of $1.1 million over three years.
  • To learn more about the study, check out this blog post.

Bring out your best now

The enhanced SAP Learning Hub provides a comprehensive learning offering for you to gain new skills, access expert guidance, and get certified, ultimately boosting your career prospects and success in a rapidly evolving technological landscape. Make sure you bring out your best and get started today.

entry.title: Customer Course GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services) Available for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
The customer course GW100 is retired and can no longer be booked as classroom. The learning journey will stay online.

The GW100 got a “technical update”, which means that all exercises are now running on the newest SAP S/4HANA available today. In addition, the slides now follow the newest design guidelines of SAP. So, welcome to this short blog post about the customer training GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services).

If you are more interested in a digital learning for SAP Gateway, please read my blog post:
SAP Learning Journey – Building OData Services with SAP Gateway

Introduction

Starting in 2014, this is now the eighth incarnation of how to develop OData services with SAP Gateway. The course shows all parts of SAP Gateway, which are available since SAP NetWeaver Application Server 7.0 (SAP Gateway 2.0), as well as parts added up to SAP S/4HANA 2023. The whole bandwidth of SAP Gateway covering all releases is shown.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. SAP Gateway Overview
  2. OData Overview
  3. SAP Gateway Service Implementation
  4. SAP Gateway Service Generation
  5. SAP Gateway Service Redefinition
  6. SAP Gateway and CDS Views
  7. SAP Gateway Hub Functionalities
  8. Advanced OData Options
  9. SAP Gateway Security
  10. Further Information

If customers are interested, they can order a customer specific version of the course including only those topics suitable for their release or purpose.

System Landscape

Let’s start with the system release: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Our system landscape – we call it universal target – is used in many technology courses and offers full access for the participants from the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP) in the browser down to SAP HANA on the SUSE Linux Enterprise server (SLES). Everything is set up following the newest guidelines of SAP so that it can really act as a template for customers.

GW100 System Landscape (Screenshot from System Setup Guide)

In addition, we offer an instance of the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) to show the consumption of OData services. If you want to know more about this topic, check out our courses for SAP Fiori development.

Example: Business Services

As an example from the material, here a shorten excerpt about defining business services.

Business Services: OData V2 and V4 (Screenshot from GW100)

In the area of business services, a service definition exposes CDS Views as a generic service. A service binding then marks it as a special kind of service. You can create an OData V2 or V4 service for API- or UI-usage. Service definitions and bindings are created in the ADT, the generated OData services are registered in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or /IWFND/V4_ADMIN respectively.

How to Create a Service Definition and Binding (Screenshot from GW100)

Service definitions and bindings are ABAP repository objects and a substantial part of the business services. They can only be created and edited in the ADT. In the first step, the service definition just defines the name of the service. The CDS views are added later in the source code.

In the second step, the service binding refers to the service definition and sets the binding type, which are mainly communication protocols.

Service Definition and Binding Correlation (Screenshot from GW100)

By exposing a CDS View in a service definition, the name of the entity set can be defined. For the entity type, "Type" is automatically appended to the entity set name.

The entity set names and navigation properties based on the associations in the CDS views are visible in the service binding on the right. For OData V2, multiple versions of a service definition can be added to a service binding visible on the left. For OData V4, multiple service definitions can be added to one service binding to allow cross-service navigation.


Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/GW100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Some parts of the GW100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/building-odata-services-with-sap-gateway

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the GW100 in any kind, you found some useful information around SAP Gateway in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: It’s All About BRIM: Join This Hybrid Event to Learn More About Billing and Revenue Management
entry.content:
If you know your way around SAP, you know there’s lots of abbreviations! But have you ever heard of BRIM? No? Then don’t worry – we’ve got you covered! Read on to learn more about BRIM and what it can do for you.

First things first. BRIM stands for “Billing and Revenue Innovation Management”. AS “Everything as a Service” is shaping the future of the global economy, companies of all sizes, from large multi-nationals to those in the rapidly growing mid-market, are evaluating their pricing models to create scalable, recurring revenues that require a shift towards long- term customer relationships. At the same time, consumers expect flexible, personalized offerings catered to them. 

Join us onsite or virtually to learn more about BRIM 

And here’s where you come in. To learn more about BRIM, you can join our hybrid SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) Forum on October 22 and 23, 2024. If you want to join virtually, you can do so via SAP Live Class. If you want to attend onsite, we’re looking forward to welcoming you onsite at the Training Center in Walldorf, Germany.  

Here’s what you can expect from the event: 

  • Benefit from first-hand expert knowledge and take the opportunity to exchange ideas with speakers from the SAP Product Management team. 
  • Listen to an exciting keynote from SAP executives on product strategies and roadmap. 
  • Join expert talks and dedicated training sessions including theoretical introductions, system demonstrations, and Q&A sessions. 
  • Meet our experts to discuss and clarify your specific questions. 
  • Engage in panel discussions on current strategic topics most relevant to your organization. 
  • Don’t neglect the entertainment factor and enjoy an evening with us in a relaxed atmosphere to expand your network. 

If you’re part of a project team, a consultant, key user, decision maker or manager – this event is for you. There’re no prerequisites, but basic knowledge in the field is recommended. Sign up today to bring out the best in you and your organization!

entry.title: SAP Learning Journey – Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Since it was announced at SAP TechEd 2021, SAP offers digital learnings free of charge to everybody interested in SAP products on https://learning.sap.com. In this blog post, I want to present to you the learning journey Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform.

 

Introduction

SAP offers plenty of services to enhance your business in one central place: SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This platform-as-a-service (PaaS) provides the central place to improve your handling of data and analytics, integrate data and processes, or develop and extend software by leveraging such SAP BTP services.

But before you can operate a service, you must define the basis like directories, subaccounts, and runtime environments. Users need authorizations and applications need connections to service provides. There are various configuration options to define your service landscape suitable for your business scenarios. All of this should also be observed and monitored concerning performance or costs.

This is exactly what this learning journey is about: Administrating the basis for operating SAP cloud services in SAP BTP. And yes, I chose the term “basis” on purpose for those people out there, who are right now on their journey from on-premise to cloud. Just take a seat in our learning journey.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance
  2. Architecture
  3. Identity and Access Management
  4. Configuration
  5. Connectivity
  6. Observability and Monitoring
  7. Cost and Usage Management

 

Content

1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance

Unit 1 describes shortly the SAP Business Technology Platform and its commercial models. Most importantly, it analyzes responsibilities and guidance resources for SAP BTP.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform acting as a prerequisite.

2. Architecture

Unit 2 covers the SAP BTP account model consisting of global accounts, directories, and subaccounts. After service subscriptions, the runtime environments in SAP BTP are managed. Cloud Foundry and Kyma are enabled and (name-) spaces created to instantiate services in these runtimes. Finaly, the ABAP environment is enabled and the landscape portal is used to manage ABAP system hibernation.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Directories
  • Create a Subaccount in a Directory
  • Create Subaccounts with Default Provider and Region
  • Entitle a Service in a Directory
  • Entitle and Subscribe a Service in a Subaccount
  • Add a Service Plan for Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Enable and Configure a Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Create a Service Instance in a Cloud Foundry
  • Add a Service Plan for Kyma Environment
  • Enable a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Namespace in a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Service Instance in Kyma
  • Entitle and Create an ABAP Environment
  • Entitle and Create Web Access for ABAP
  • Create a Subaccount for Operation
  • Entitle, Create, and Authorize the Landscape Portal
  • Check ABAP System Details and Manage System Hibernation

3. Identity and Access Management

Unit 3 explains the identity and access management on SAP BTP on all levels and distinguishes business from platform users. How to manage users and authorizations on SAP BTP  with roles and role collections connected to identity providers is followed by a summary of the security concept for cloud native applications. This leads to the management of users and authorizations in SAP BTP environments.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Administrator User in a Subaccount
  • Inspect and Assign Predefined Role Collections for Applications
  • Assign Developer Authorizations in Cloud Foundry
  • Manage Cluster Roles in Kyma
  • Manage Business Roles in the ABAP Environment

4. Configuration

Unit 4 provides various ways to configure the SAP BTP starting with boosters, which are guided interactive steps to configure services. Operating the command line interface (CLI) tools btp CLI for SAP BTP and cf CLI for Cloud Foundry are like the kubectl for Kyma the most direct way to interact with SAP BTP on a technical level. To manage SAP BTP with Terraform incorporates btp CLI to automate infrastructure provisioning and management.

The following is exercised:

  • Start a Booster in SAP BTP
  • Operate SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI)
  • Setting and Configuring Kubectl for Kyma
  • Create a Terraform Configuration for SAP BTP

5. Connectivity

Unit 5 starts with analyzing connectivity in SAP BTP and jumps to using destinations on subaccount, service, and environment level. To connect the SAP BTP to on-premise systems, operating the cloud connector is mandatory for any SAP BTP service.

The following is exercised:

  • Create a Destination
  • How to Install Cloud Connector on Linux
  • Replace the UI Certificate in Cloud Connector
  • How to Configure First SAP BTP Subaccount in Cloud Connector
  • Configure Access Control (HTTP)
  • How to Use a Cloud to On-Premise Scenario

6. Observability and Monitoring

Unit 6 imparts the observability strategy of SAP BTP with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) in its core. It offers central observability tools like business process, integration, user, or health monitoring. Including the local observability tools SAP Alert Notification Service and SAP Automation Pilot, SAP BTP offers a comprehensive monitoring approach.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Operating with SAP Cloud ALM, especially unit 2 (Using Monitoring and Analysis).

7. Cost and Usage Management

Unit 7 closes the learning journey with cost and usage management, a topic often requested by customers.

There is no exercise in this unit but many slides and videos with hints around handling the costs of SAP BTP efficiently.

 

Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about consuming the learning journey. Don’t hesitate and jump right in:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/administrating-sap-business-technology-platform

If you want to put your hands on some live system, the SAP BTP trial is just on click away:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/pricing.html

If you are more interested in a customer course for SAP BTP administration, please visit https://training.sap.com. The ADMCLD (Introduction to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Administration) is a 3-day-course offered as physical and virtual event:

https://training.sap.com/course/admcld

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Breaking Out of Comfort Zones: The Learning Journey of Danielle Larocca
entry.content:
There is no doubt that the way we learn today has changed – dramatically. “I remember what it was like to attend SAP training classes back in the late 90’s”, Danielle Larocca recounts, “back then they were hosted onsite only and you would get paper-based certificates via a small ceremony each time.”  

It was 1995, and for Danielle, her SAP HCM and Payroll reporting experience was just about to start. She was working for Doubleday Direct, which later became a Bertelsmann AG and AOL Time Warner Partnership in Garden City, New York. The company had decided to implement SAP amidst fears that their legacy payroll system would cease functioning in the Y2K era. And as part of the implementation project, she was sent off for ABAP/4 training.  

Learning all about AI

Today, the learning experience looks very different for the global VP for Strategic Alliances. “Over the past several months, I have been dedicating my educational hours to all things AI.” What else would it be in the year 2024? The SAP Mentor started writing a series of blogs, ranging from AI-generated headshots, to functional video avatars and video language conversions. Her work – and learning experience – has become way more digital, fast-paced, and versatile compared to previous decades. “I have even let AI write a blog post for me and make a ppt for me", she highlights. “I also make an effort to share a weekly #AI Byte on LinkedIn that shares with others some key topics on AI that should be on their radar.” No doubt that the way she learns and the topics she learns about have changed.  

But there is more to is, says Danielle: “My life has changed in the sense that I have gone outside my comfort zone.” Moving away from HCM, Payroll, and reporting – her familiar turf – Danielle started to learn about technologies outside of her world, “to expand my horizon on the art of the possible with what technology can do.” Her latest achievement: The “Introducing SAP Business AI Commercial Model” learning journey on SAP Learning site, which rewarded her with an official record of achievement to demonstrate her expertise. 

Adapting to changing technologies is important

However, Danielle admits that learning a new skill sometimes comes with a small damper: “It is hard to A) carve out the time and B) learn something new but it is rewarding and exciting to be in the loop on the latest technological advancements that will transform our everyday lives.” As we get older, she states, “it's easy to settle into our homeostasis of what we know and what we are experts on and it's easy to stay in that comfort zone, but it does not allow for personal growth.” According to Danielle, we all must adapt and learn new things to survive and thrive, especially in an ever-changing technological evolution climate.   

“With all that said I have carved out time from my very busy schedule to learn something new each week.  I call it my education hours and they usually fall on a Sunday night.  I am committed to learning something new for 2 hours every week that is outside the scope of my day-to-day life and it involves some form of cutting-edge tech, for me its AI.” 

How do you learn? Share!

How do you tackle learning a new skill? Let us know about your strategy in the comments and start your next chapter of boosting your career with future-focused learning on the SAP Learning site today.

entry.title: Come Say Hi at an SAP TechEd Tour Stop
entry.content:
It’s a wrap! Yes, sadly, SAP TechEd Virtual is already over, but it’s been a blast! But the good news is: If you want to continue to learn, network, and bring out your best, you can still participate in one of five SAP TechEd on Tour or SAPinsider events worldwide – with learning sessions and meet-ups with SAP experts. Here’s what you need to know to come and say “Hi”: 

SAP is offering five separate tour stops around the globe, organized in collaboration with SAP user groups. Each stop will offer hands-on learning opportunities, deep-dive lecture sessions, and meet-the-experts all presented by SAP experts, partners, and many customers. We are also bringing back the Developer Garage for you as a great opportunity to learn by doing. 

Choose the US or Europe to meet SAP experts 

From November 12-14, join technical practitioners and SAP experts at ASUG Tech Connect 2024 in West Palm Beach for immersive sessions, SAP insights, and networking. Over three days, you'll enhance your skills, stay at the forefront of digital advancements, and grow professionally. 

If you prefer a trip to Europe, SAPinsider EMEA 2024 in Copenhagen, Denmark, is for you. From November 12-14, you can develop the skills and expertise needed to thrive in your role. Hear from customers about their successful digital transformations, learn implementation best practices from SAP partners, and connect with peers and SAP experts in a three-day learning event aligned with SAP TechEd Virtual.  

Get an overview of the SAP ecosystem in Australia 

If you fancy a trip to Down Under, we’re excited to meet you at SAUG-x 2024 this year! From November 19-20 you can learn from unique insights, network with your peers, hear from SAP experts, and get an overview of our ecosystem. Don’t miss these relevant knowledge sharing, learning, and networking sessions – all in Melbourne, Australia! 

And if you’re still in the mood for SAP TechEd by December, join us in Birmingham, UK, at UKISUG Connect 2024. At the ICC in Birmingham, expect to learn from experts, network with like-minded peers and collaborate together. Benefit from knowledge-rich keynotes, engaging breakout sessions, a range of workshops and an exhibition hall of over 70 SAP partners. 

Last, but not least, head over to the motherland of SAP – Germany – and join us at DSAG-TechXchange 2025 on April 4, 2025! Yes, it might be a couple of months away, but having your next trip planned is always a nice thing, don’t you think? 

Whichever location you prefer – we are looking forward to seeing you there! Make use of the unique opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, gain valuable insights into the tech industry, and get hands-on experience with the latest SAP technologies. Whether you're a developer, architect, IT manager, or business executive, SAP TechEd on Tour has something for everyone. Don't miss out on this opportunity to stay ahead of the curve and propel your tech career to new heights! 

For more information on the SAP TechEd on Tour stops, visit this page. 

And while you’re at it, make sure to attend SAP CodeJam events in your area - free, hands-on coding experiences where you can collaborate with fellow developers to solve real-world challenges while diving deep into the latest SAP technologies.

entry.title: Kickstart your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Transformation Consultant
entry.content:
In a fast-paced world where companies rely on adaptation and transformation to have success, the role of the solution transformation consultant takes center stage. SAP Solution Transformation Consultants are responsible for guiding the entire business transformation journey, from initial design to final deployment, using a comprehensive end-to-end toolchain. They play a crucial role in accelerating transformation projects by leveraging best practices and ensuring high-quality business outcomes.

They navigate through digital landscapes and guide customers on a transformative journey from traditional ERP systems to the innovative SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Envision playing a central role in the RISE with SAP initiative, empowering enterprises to surge ahead in their market spaces. And with this brand-new role-based certification, we are helping you to achieve this goal.

Are you ready to shape the future of business transformation?

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM. This certification validates that you possess the application lifecycle management skills and core knowledge in SAP Cloud ALM required for the consultant profile. By getting certified, you demonstrate that you have a comprehensive understanding, and the in-depth skills required to lead a project team as a solution transformation consultant, guiding the team through the solution transformation journey using SAP Cloud ALM. 

Start with this free learning journey

To get started, take the new free, self-paced learning journey "Transforming for Success: Solution Transformation with SAP Cloud ALM" on the SAP Learning site.  In just 16 hours – divided into six sections– you will learn how SAP Cloud ALM, as part of SAP's transformation approach, supports the entire solution transformation journey—from business and solution transformation methodology to agile implementation and efficient operations. After completing this learning journey, you will have the skills to leverage SAP Cloud ALM for comprehensive solution transformation and have an overview of SAP's business transformation strategy.

If you want to learn more about how SAP can help you accelerate digital transformation, check out the story of Martina Ohlerth and benefit from her first-hand experience!

Certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification.  If you want to know more on how to get and stay certified, you can read this blog post.

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and get inspired by reading learner stories on SAP Community, ask questions and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: Coach's Corner: The Essential Act of Receiving
entry.content:
When organizations start out on the path of creating a “Feedback Culture,” they are so focused on the delivery methodology that they forget the important other-half of the equation – receiving.

 

Give. Give. Give.

It’s no wonder. In the West especially, we are instructed by faith leaders to “Give, and you will receive.” Since we were young, we were taught by parents and teachers to share, because “sharing means caring.” In school, we were expected to give our opinions, ask questions and share ideas as part of the learning process. We are encouraged to give compliments because this will result in making friends and keeping neighbours happy. In life, this ‘Pay It Forward’ mindset spawns an entire industry of charities and crowd-funding outlets for doing-good. Finally, at work, we are expected to join training programs and learn how to give feedback to our colleagues, our managers, our suppliers and even our customers.

 

To receive.

By contrast, in Eastern societies, children are taught to receive. I mean receiving in the way of accepting, and want to distinguish this from ‘taking.’ The main role of children in the East is to receive the resources, opportunities and energy of their parents and elders, and to focus on studying and preparing for the future. From a very young age, they were taught to listen in class and receive the knowledge of their teachers. When it comes to work and life, the practice of receiving shows up as acceptance and common understanding of each person’s role in a team and in an organization. The Taoist tenet to “be like water” shapes the values, and therefore the mindset and behaviours of many in Eastern society.

 

We seem to have an easier time giving feedback, advice and opinions, yet struggle with receiving them when they are offered to us. See my post “Feedback: The Gift That’s Hard to Accept” for reasons why we struggle with this. One action is not better than the other. However, without the corresponding practice of receiving there would be no value in the feedback. By receiving and accepting someone’s gift, we also honour them and fulfill their good intentions. The combination of both giving and receiving completes the equation.  

 

How do we become better at receiving?

  • Be present – Preparing our mind, our breath, and our emotions isn’t just reserved for delivering presentations or giving bad news. We must also prepare ourselves to receive, by calming our mind and focusing on the person we’re with. If you’re not in a state to receive, then you’re not going to get the maximum benefit of the gift.

  • Assume best intentions – Our biases and interpretations stand in the way of learning. Focus on the content and the words to remove biases of who may be delivering the message. This also means keeping your ego in check – even the most junior member of your team or the most unexpected source can offer you value.

  • Be curious and ask questions – Some of us need to overcome our default to only give, while others may need to strengthen our propensity to receive. Our ability to receive can be learned and reinforced by asking simple questions. For example, “Would you be OK if I asked for some more feedback in the future?” 

 

In our life, there will be times when we see things as opposing each other – ebb and flow; push and pull; Yin and Yang; workers and management. When we choose to look at these forces as part of the same system, they are actually complimentary. In developing a Culture of Feedback, we need to be as good at receiving as we are at giving.

 

[Reposted from the Stone Soup Coaching blog]

entry.title: Education, Determination, and Tech: How Pegah Integrated and Succeeded
entry.content:
“My way to set foot in Germany was through education – that's how I integrated.” When speaking about her journey from a refugee to a woman in tech @ SAP – that’s how Pegah Eghbali Alamdari titled one of her speaking engagements – you would have never guessed that she struggled with public speaking. “This fear was always there, like a shadow.” But today feels different for the 33-year-old, who always dreamt of working at SAP. 

Pegah and her sister were born in Iran, but her parents early on decided that their girls shouldn’t grow up in their home country. When the family fled, Pegah was merely seven years old. “My father went ahead, he wanted to get us to Germany quickly, but that didn't work out right away, so Pegah, her sister, and their mother made a two-month journey to Germany”, she explains. And when they were finally reunited, life looked very different suddenly: “new culture, new language, new people”, Pegah says. 

SAP was the big goal

But one thing she recognized, felt comfortable with, was math. “As a universal language, math was one of the few things I knew from my previous life and understood without needing to speak the language. That’s what I knew and how I could show what I was capable of. Thanks to my achievements in math, I was able to belong.” She graduated with success and went on to study medical informatics. “And when I studied, the big goal was SAP.”  

Pegah and her sister were born in Iran, but her parents early on decided that their girls shouldn’t grow up in their home country.Four years ago, she was hired and now works as a Senior Technology Consultant in Cybersecurity and Compliance. “Our team ensures that our customers' S4HANA systems are secure and compliant”, she explains, adding that her expertise is in authorizations. “I help customers by developing secure authorization concepts for their S4HANA systems. I am rarely on-site with customers, which allows me to work very flexibly and support multiple projects at the same time.” 

"You have to face your fears"

But besides projects at work, there’s also other projects close to her heart: As a volunteer, Pegah taught math in Zanzibar (Tansania), passing on what was her path to success: education. “I want to show everyone that you can be successful and that you can do it, if you want it.” According to her, hard work and passion are essential to make dreams come true. “You have to face your fears, put the goal above everything else”, she advises. Adding: “And you must be prepared to learn something new.” 

Pegah shares, "I feel like life moves really quickly. To not fall behind tomorrow, it's important to realize that life itself is a school. If we stop learning, we'll eventually just stay in one place. I know I love learning, so I want to absorb as much as I can to move forward with the best." Learning about secure authorization concepts for SAP systems has allowed her to be more efficient and provide valuable support to her customers. Pegah reflects on her journey, saying, "I have received an important voice which means to have the power to make a difference". 

Started a non-profit organization

In addition to professional growth, the knowledge she has gained empowered her to give back to the community. Pegah has started with her sister and two other SAP colleagues (Cigdem Guendogdu and Dilara Yarar) a non-profit organization names “BLESSMANITY Germany e.V.” that focuses on improving access to education for children in developing countries. Currently they are building a canteen at a kindergarten in Zanzibar to ensure that the children receive a nutritious meal once a day. The skills she has mastered have given her the confidence and capability to make a difference in the lives of those in need.  

For Pegah, learning new skills has been life-changing, giving her a sense of purpose and the power to create a positive impact. Pegah shares, "I want to inspire people and be a role model, showing that integration and success are possible through education, determination, discipline, and courage. Anything is possible in life. I am very grateful for everything I have received in life and see it as my duty to give back." 

Learning offers life-changing potential

Her personal story is a testament to the countless benefits of learning and growing. Whether it's professional growth, work-life balance, or the ability to make a difference, the advantages are significant and impactful. 

If you’re considering learning and growing, Pegah’s story is a compelling reminder of the life-changing potential it holds. Whether it’s for professional advancement or personal fulfilment, learning SAP skills could also be the right path for you. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Please ask a question in the Q&A area
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.


There’s no doubt about the tangible and compelling benefits of an IT certification. According to the “2023 Value of IT Certification Candidate Report” by Pearson VUE, 79% of certified individuals received or anticipated a salary increase after getting certified. 81% of them produced a higher quality of work – besides the fact that 92% felt more confident in their abilities. So, getting certified makes sense.  

But how do you get certified in the first place? And more importantly: How do you stay certified? You’ll find out in this blog post! 

Let’s start with the basics: A new certification program and offering 

The demand for skilled IT professionals is expected to grow dramatically – up 23% by 2031 in the United States alone, according to the U.S. Department of Labor. This pace of change and the talent shortage call for a new approach to identifying future-focused skills and the necessity of staying up to date. To help meet these new circumstances, SAP enhanced its certification program in the beginning of 2024 with the launch of the enhanced SAP Learning Hub.

One subscription for all certification resources 

The great thing about the enhanced SAP Learning Hub is that you can both get and stay certified with one single subscription to the premium, guided learning offering. Once subscribed, you get access to future-focused learning content, expert guidance in live sessions, on-demand access to hands-on practice systems, and enhanced analytics.  

When you’re ready to get certified, you don’t need to worry about buying certification attempts like in the past: Four attempts per year are already included and once successfully passed, your SAP Certification is valid for 12 months! You can then continuously expand the validity of your certification by passing the yearly assessments to stay certified. 

Can I still buy single certification attempts? 

Don’t worry – if you wanted to buy one or six SAP Certification attempts like in the past – you still can. But there are some things you should consider: You can still complete all your learning, successfully pass the exam, and demonstrate your expertise with your badge for 12 months.  

However, after one year, you will need to re-certify – leaving you with two options: 1. Subscribing to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub, where stay certified assessments are included or 2. buying a new SAP Certification exam attempt. But that would mean you had to retake the full certification exam as well.  

What’s the difference between the SAP Certification exam and the stay certified assessment? 

Great that you ask – because the difference is big! As said, as a subscriber to SAP Learning Hub, your annual stay certified assessments are included in your subscription. But in contrast to classic SAP Certification exams, stay certified assessments are short, not proctored quizzes with unlimited trials. They test only the knowledge between releases to make sure learners stay continuously up to date with the solution updates. Plus: To prepare for your assessment, you have access to dedicated learning materials, like live sessions.  

The rules for an SAP Certification exam are different: The exams are proctored and can typically take up to three hours. Candidates are required to answer 40-80 questions, out of which 60-70% correctly to pass the exam. If candidates fail the exam, they need to repurchase an SAP Certification exam attempt and retake the exam. 

So, what’s the recommendation here? 

Of course, this totally depends on your role and personal learning preferences. However, we do see the benefits of opting for the SAP Learning Hub subscription. With one single subscription, you can get access to all resources you need to prepare for certification, take the exams with four included attempts and stay certified – at better value than ever before.  

To learn more about the ways to get and stay certified, visit this FAQ and let us know in the comments about the benefits you had thanks to SAP Certifications!

entry.title: Empowering Your SAP Journey: Insights from SAP Champions and Mentors
entry.content:
As tech professionals, we understand the importance of staying ahead in the constantly evolving tech industry. Thanks to the cloud, SAP is innovating every day – and we need (and want to) keep up with the speed. That’s not only true for newcomers in the field, but also for well-versed professionals like SAP Mentors and SAP Champions. Listen to the top influencers, contributors, and advocates within the SAP ecosystem and learn why you should invest in continuous learning, SAP skills, and certification now, with this new video series: 

The first video, "Stay Ahead with Certifications and Continuous Learning," features SAP Mentors emphasizing the importance of continuous learning and certifications in keeping up with the fast-paced tech industry. They share their own experiences and insights on how SAP Certifications have helped them stay ahead in their careers.

"Empowering Your Digital Transformation with SAP" delves into the topic of digital transformation and how SAP is at the forefront of enabling companies to prepare for and embrace this transformation. The SAP Mentors share their insights on the importance of aligning with SAP's digital transformation strategy and how it can drive success for businesses in today's digital age.

In the next video, "Leveraging AI Resources to Drive Innovation and Efficiency," SAP Mentors discuss the role of AI in driving innovation and increasing efficiency within the SAP ecosystem, providing valuable advice on leveraging these resources effectively.  

To wrap up, "Unlocking Potential with SAP Learning Hub" focuses on the power of SAP Learning Hub in providing valuable resources and training for tech professionals. The Mentors share their thoughts on how SAP Learning Hub has played a crucial role in their professional development and how it can benefit others in the SAP community.  

In the words of our SAP Mentors: 

“SAP Certifications have never been easier to get. Now you simply just go to learning.sap.com and you can access all of the learning content.“ 
– @DanielleLarocca, SAP Partner, Strategic Alliances, EPI-USE Labs 

“One of the most important things to our customers and partners is to keep up with all this latest innovation. That is why it’s so important to take a look at the new learning journey around the SAP Business AI portfolio.“ 
– @DiegoDora, SAP Partner, Founder, CEO and Chief Architect at Diff Consulting 

Start your own success story today and head over to SAP Learning site now!

entry.title: Coach's Corner - Be Water and Let Your Career Flow
entry.content:
The phrase "Be water" is most famously associated with Bruce Lee, the martial artist, movie star and philosopher. He shared this idea during an interview in 1971, where he explained the concept of adaptability and flexibility. His full quote is:

"Empty your mind, be formless, shapeless—like water. Now you put water in a cup, it becomes the cup; You put water into a bottle, it becomes the bottle; You put it in a teapot, it becomes the teapot. Now water can flow or it can crash. Be water, my friend."

(image generated by AI)

 
Bruce Lee was influenced by Taoism. Taoism, particularly through its foundational text, the Tao Te Ching by Laozi, heavily emphasizes the concept of "being like water" and "going with the flow." This philosophy emphasizes being adaptable, flexible, and able to adjust to different circumstances, much like water takes the shape of whatever contains it.
 
The parallel I'd like to draw between our careers and being like water comes from a recent conversation I had with a senior recruiter at SAP. One of the most challenging part of his job was to explain to job seekers (especially young applicants within the same organization) why they weren't picked to interview for a position that was 2 or 3 pay-grades higher than their current role. Getting an interview in this circumstance is rare, and the chances of getting the job is even slimmer. His advice for job seekers is to have the right expectations and get to know the process. This advice is basic but essential, and isn't unique to SAP but applies to almost all seeking to progress upwards in any organization. 
 
Every organization has a flow - a rhythm, cycle or way of doing things. It has established processes, schedules and systems to regulate hiring, assess skills, provide training, manage performance, distribute pay, promotions and raises, and plan for leadership succession. By knowing what the rhythm is and adapting your expectations and actions to fit this flow, you will not only be more successful but also encounter greater satisfaction with your career trajectory. 
 
If you've ever done the opposite and tried to go against the established process within a company, you'll know it is like swimming against a river's current. The likely outcome is greater fatigue and missing your target destination. Job seekers and employees who expect and push for changes off-cycle to the company's flow often end up exhausted and frustrated with the results. Sure, it's possible for an intern to become CEO in the same organization, but the chances are miniscule and I usually advise a more pragmatic and achievable goal for my clients that involves manageable and realistic steps towards the aspirational goal.

Be Content Without Being Complacent

 
While "going with the flow" inherently requires trust and patience with the system we're in, there is also a large dose of appreciation and contentment we must learn to adopt within ourselves. The pull-through benefit of adopting this approach is the reduction of stress and pressure we feel from unrealistic expectations (whether created by ourselves or by others) and better management of our scarcity mindset.
 
Being content with our current condition does not mean being stagnant in pursuing our personal aspirations and career growth. To move from a 'scarcity mindset' to an 'abundance mindset,' we must proactively manage our thoughts while intentionally managing our behaviours.
 
Here are 3 ways to remain content without being complacent:
  1. Continuous learning - One of the first things to learn is the timing and schedule of relevant systems in your organization or the organization you're interested in working for. The goal is to understand how processes such as hiring, promotions, transfers and succession planning work in the organization. I would advocate spending time adapting your approach to the organizational flow, rather than trying to find back-doors, exceptions and ways to game the system.
  2. Build your network and relationships - The best way I can recommend learning about a company's flow is from your network. This means building new connections, reconnect with those you've loss touch with, and maintaining your existing relationships. Even though career networking sites (like Glassdoor and LinkedIn) are available, speaking directly to your network of friends and acquaintances is the fastest and most trusted way to learn about a company's flow. Stay curious and ask lots of questions, and you might find you're actually pretty good at it.
  3. Find help - Whether it's a coach or a mentor, you can fast-track your efforts when you have a trusted advisor close by to support and sanity-check your plan. A good coach or mentor will not let you forget your strengths and accomplishments, and will encourage you to apply those experiences to achieve your short term and long term goals. They will also help you take stock of your achievements and put expectations into perspective. Ask for help to develop a strategy and tactics to use the company's flow to get you across faster, safely and on-track to reach your career destination.
Embracing the philosophy of "being like water" in your career means adapting to your organization's flow, understanding its processes, and aligning your expectations with its rhythm. By doing so, you'll not only increase your chances of success but also find greater satisfaction in your career journey. Remember, being content doesn't mean being complacent. Continuous learning, building your network, and seeking help from mentors or coaches are proactive ways to maintain an abundance mindset and propel your career forward. So, be water, my friend, and let your career flow.

(Blog reposted from stonesoupcoaching.wordpress.com )
